liveSession.label: Live Saturday route
liveSession.summary: A recurring session already sits inside this support pillar, so the programme page can explain the theme while the session page handles timings and joining details.

sessionLimited.label: Session details vary
sessionLimited.summary: This support pillar already maps to live delivery, but the clearest next date may need confirming through the related session route or contact.

overviewOnly.label: Programme overview
overviewOnly.summary: This support area is part of the launch offer now, even where the delivery format is still described best through the programme overview and support conversation.

enquiryOnly.label: Enquiry-led route
enquiryOnly.summary: This support direction is public now, but current access starts with a conversation rather than a published recurring timetable.

atAGlanceSection.title: The essentials are visible before anyone has to decode the route.
atAGlanceSection.summary: Each programme page explains who it is for, how it currently shows up, and what kind of next step makes sense.

contentSection.title: What this programme focuses on right now.

relatedSessionsSection.title: Use the clearest route available right now.
relatedSessionsSection.summary: Some programmes point to a live recurring session. Others start with a lighter enquiry route until more delivery detail is ready to publish.

trustSection.title: Trust cues stay close to the programme, not buried in general site copy.
trustSection.summary: Each programme page carries its own reassurance about welcome, privacy, safeguarding, or credibility boundaries where relevant.

ctaBand.title: Move from the programme overview into a real route.
ctaBand.summary: If a live session exists, use it for timings and first-visit detail. If not, use the contact route and the team can help you work out the right next step.

migrationNotice.title: Launch note
migrationNotice.body: Some programme pages still need fuller examples, logistics, and trust detail. Keep the copy practical and avoid filling those gaps with assumptions.

hero.title: Speak to people, join in at your pace, and feel comfortable coming back.
hero.summary: Community & Friendship is the route for games, conversation, low-pressure friendship, and regular connection without expecting someone to arrive already confident.
hero.supportingNote: Based in Rochdale. The linked youth club session is the clearest live route into this programme, and venue details can be shared when someone enquires.

experienceSection.title: The first experience should feel welcoming, not intimidating.
experienceSection.summary: This route is designed to lower the pressure: young people can settle in, join games, talk, or simply get used to the room before they feel fully involved.

items.title: Start without pressure
items.summary: No one needs to prove themselves before they are welcomed, given time to settle, or invited to join in at their own pace.

items.title: Games and conversation with room to settle
items.summary: Games, conversation, and chill time create more than one way to take part, so the route does not depend on instant confidence.

items.title: Friendship that makes returning easier
items.summary: The goal is regular connection and familiarity, so coming back can feel easier as people recognise faces and routines over time.

relatedSessionsSection.title: Youth club is the clearest live expression of this programme.
relatedSessionsSection.summary: The programme page explains the belonging-first offer. The youth club session page carries exact timing, calendar access, what to expect, and first-visit logistics.

panels.title: Why this page still matters
panels.body: This route explains the wider promise of games, conversation, low-pressure friendship, and repeat connection so visitors do not have to infer it from a timetable alone.

panels.title: Venue details and first contact
panels.body: Rochdale stays the public location anchor. Specific venue details can be shared on enquiry so first-time visitors can ask questions before attending.

activeNotice.title: Youth club is currently the live route
activeNotice.body: If someone is ready for the practical next step, use the youth club session page for exact timing, calendar details, and what to expect. This programme page still matters because it explains the wider belonging-first promise behind that session.

fallbackNotice.title: Need the next youth club date confirmed?
fallbackNotice.body: If the live session timing changes, use the contact route to confirm the next date or ask whether this programme is still the right fit.

faqSection.title: Answer the first belonging and safety questions before anyone arrives.

evidenceNotice.title: Public-proof boundary
evidenceNotice.body: This page keeps to the public essentials. Named participant stories, venue photography, and team-profile proof stay out until they are approved for publication.

hero.title: Practise real-life confidence, motivation, and everyday next steps.
hero.summary: Personal Growth & Life Skills is for young people who want help speaking up, building motivation, handling everyday situations, and choosing a realistic next step.
hero.supportingNote: This offer is live at programme level now. Specific workshop formats, dates, or mentor arrangements are not all published yet, so the clearest next step is a short contact message.

experienceSection.eyebrow: What support here can feel like
experienceSection.title: Growth support that feels practical, encouraging, and possible to step into.
experienceSection.summary: The page helps someone picture support with confidence, motivation, everyday skills, and next steps without pretending every detail is already a public timetable.

items.title: Speaking up without pressure
items.summary: Someone can ask for help, practise speaking up, and take part without feeling judged for not having everything sorted out yet.

items.title: Everyday skills and motivation
items.summary: The public promise covers practical development, motivation, and skills that help someone handle everyday situations and future opportunities.

items.title: Guidance toward a realistic next step
items.summary: This route can include mentoring-style encouragement, workshop learning, or practical guidance, while the exact current format is confirmed honestly on enquiry.

relatedSessionsSection.title: Start with the programme overview and a short conversation.
relatedSessionsSection.summary: This route is public because the support direction is real. Dates, workshop rhythms, or named formats should only appear once they are confirmed for publication.

panels.title: What this support can include
panels.body: Support can include real-life confidence, life-skills development, mentoring, motivation, and practical guidance. The public promise stays broad enough to stay truthful while still helping someone recognise the fit.

panels.title: Why contact comes first
panels.body: The right next step may depend on what support someone needs now. A short enquiry lets the team explain whether the best fit is a conversation, a workshop-style opportunity, or another live route already on the site.

panels.title: Need something scheduled today?
panels.body: If someone wants an immediate practical or community route, the live Sessions area stays the clearest public place to start while this programme keeps its schedule detail intentionally light.

overviewNotice.title: Programme live, public timing still intentionally light
overviewNotice.body: This page explains what the support is for. Use contact if you want to ask whether confidence-building support, workshop-style learning, or another current route is the best fit right now.

emptyState.title: Use a short enquiry to work out the right next step.
emptyState.summary: The offer is real, but the launch site does not yet publish a separate recurring timetable for this programme. Contact the team and they can guide you toward the right conversation, activity, or live session.

faqSection.eyebrow: Questions before someone asks for help
faqSection.title: The page should answer the first practical questions before anyone has to explain themselves.

evidenceNotice.title: Public-proof boundary
evidenceNotice.body: This page stays focused on the public essentials: confidence-building, life skills, mentoring, and practical guidance. Public dates, mentor profiles, outcome claims, or workshop photography stay out until they are approved for publication.

hero.title: Get help with CVs, applications, interviews, and next steps.
hero.summary: Career Support & CV Help is the wider practical-support route for people who need help with a CV draft, an unfinished application, interview questions, work or college next steps, or getting started.
hero.supportingNote: The Saturday CV support session is the clearest live route into this programme. Use that page for exact timing, calendar access, what to bring, and how to ask before attending; use this page to understand the wider support promise.

experienceSection.eyebrow: What practical support can feel like
experienceSection.title: The route should feel useful straight away, not intimidating.
experienceSection.summary: Someone can recognise the fit of the support before they have a polished CV or a perfect plan.

items.title: Bring what you already have
items.summary: A draft CV, an unfinished application, an interview question, or no document yet is enough to start. The programme leaves room for unfinished work and first questions.

items.title: Practical help that turns into action
items.summary: The public promise is about usable progress: wording, structure, interview preparation, next steps, and guidance someone can apply after the session rather than vague motivation alone.

items.title: Confidence grows through clarity
items.summary: Career support here is not just about documents. It helps people feel more ready to ask questions, apply, prepare for interviews, and take the next step with support around them.

relatedSessionsSection.title: CV support is the clearest live expression of this programme.
relatedSessionsSection.summary: This page explains the wider practical-support route. The CV support session page carries the every-Saturday 16:45 timing, 120-minute duration, calendar access, and first-visit detail.

panels.title: Why this page still matters
panels.body: This route helps someone recognise that CV help, application support, interview preparation, and practical next-step guidance fit them before they have to decode a timetable or assume the session is only about one document.

panels.title: What the session page owns
panels.body: The linked CV support session carries the exact recurrence, add-to-calendar access, and attendance expectations like what to bring or how to ask first. That keeps the programme page from duplicating live logistics.

panels.title: Not sure whether to attend yet?
panels.body: Use contact if someone needs to ask whether the live session is right for them, wants to check the next date, or needs broader guidance before attending in person.

activeNotice.title: CV support is the live route right now
activeNotice.body: If someone wants direct practical help, use the CV support session page for the every-Saturday 16:45 timing, 120-minute duration, calendar file, and first-visit detail. This programme page still matters because it frames the wider career-support offer around that session.

fallbackNotice.title: Need the next CV support date confirmed?
fallbackNotice.body: If the live session timing changes or pauses, use contact to check the next date or ask whether the wider career-support route is still the right fit.

faqSection.title: Answer the first practical questions before anyone has to arrive with everything sorted.

ctaBand.title: Use the route that matches what someone needs right now.
ctaBand.summary: Go straight to the live CV support session if you want practical help on Saturday. Use contact if you want to ask first, bring an unfinished draft, or need help deciding whether the live session is the right fit.

evidenceNotice.title: Public-proof boundary
evidenceNotice.body: This page stays focused on the public essentials: CV help, applications support, interview preparation, work or college next steps, and next-step guidance. Employment placements, guaranteed outcomes, named advisers, or wider coaching models stay out until they are approved for publication.

hero.title: Bringing people together across generations through support and connection.
hero.summary: Community Support / Intergenerational Connection is the wider welcome route for people who may need company, gentle practical help, or a clearer first conversation about what support, community activity, or next step might fit.
hero.supportingNote: EncouragingYou remains youth-led, but the welcome is wider than youth-only support. Public access currently starts with a short enquiry rather than a standing open timetable, and Rochdale stays the public location anchor.

experienceSection.eyebrow: What support here can feel like
experienceSection.title: A welcoming, practical route that starts with conversation.
experienceSection.summary: The page needs enough shape to feel trustworthy even while event timing and venue detail stay enquiry-led.

items.title: Connection without pressure
items.summary: People can ask about social connection, shared activities, or a first conversation without feeling they are committing to something they do not yet understand.

items.title: Community-rooted support
items.summary: The public promise covers community events, social gatherings, and practical help that bring people together locally rather than treating support as a distant service.

items.title: A calm first conversation
items.summary: Family members, carers, or referrers can ask first, explain what is needed briefly, and be guided toward the right next step.

audienceRoutesSection.title: Different visitors need different next steps.
audienceRoutesSection.summary: This page broadens the welcome while still helping people choose the clearest path instead of collapsing everything into one community label.

items.title: Looking for connection or wider community support
items.summary: Use contact if someone wants to ask about community events, intergenerational connection, or support that might reduce isolation.

items.title: Asking on behalf of a parent, relative, or someone who feels isolated
items.summary: A short message is the safest first step when a carer, family member, or referrer wants to explain what support might help before anyone attends.

items.title: Looking for a published live activity right now
items.summary: If you want the clearest public timetable today, the Sessions hub is a better starting point than this enquiry-led route.

items.title: Want to help shape or support the wider offer
items.summary: Use Get involved if you are here to volunteer, partner, or explore community collaboration rather than ask for support yourself.

relatedSessionsSection.title: Start with contact, then use the clearest live route if needed.
relatedSessionsSection.summary: This programme is public now, but it is not presented as a standing events portal. The team can explain whether the right next step is a conversation, a community event, or another route already live on the site.

panels.title: What this page owns
panels.body: It explains the wider promise: cross-age connection, community-rooted support, and a welcome that includes older or isolated people without losing the youth-led identity of the organisation.

panels.title: How community events are shared
panels.body: Occasional community events or gatherings can be published as updates when ready, but the launch site does not pretend there is always a live open timetable on this route.

panels.title: What this route is not
panels.body: This page does not describe regulated care, home visits, or formal elderly-services provision. It is a community-support and connection route that starts with conversation and fit.

enquiryNotice.title: Start with a short support conversation
enquiryNotice.body: Use contact if you want to ask whether this route fits, whether a community event is coming up, or whether another current page on the site is the better first step. Specific venue or access detail can be shared on enquiry.

emptyState.title: Use contact to understand the right next step.
emptyState.summary: This route is intentionally broader than one recurring session. The team can explain whether the next step is a conversation, an occasional community event, a live session, or another support path on the site.

faqSection.title: Answer the first fit and trust questions before anyone has to explain themselves in detail.

ctaBand.title: Move into the right conversation, live route, or support pathway.
ctaBand.summary: Use contact if you want to ask about wider community support or intergenerational connection. Use live sessions if you need a published timetable today, and use Get involved if you want to support the work.

note.title: Need to raise a concern?
note.body: If your message is about safety or wellbeing rather than a general support enquiry, use the safeguarding route so the concern reaches the right place.

evidenceNotice.title: Public-proof boundary
evidenceNotice.body: This page stays focused on the public essentials: wider community support, community events, and support for older or isolated people who may need connection. Regulated care claims, transport or home-visit promises, named partner referrals, or real-participant photography stay out until they are approved for publication.
